title: QUIC Interop Retry Child-Process Work Item
summary: Define and land the smallest child-process Retry contract that proves the server issues one valid Retry, the client replays exactly once, the managed handshake completes on the real harness path, and the runner-generated single HTTP/0.9 request reaches byte delivery plus EOF.
planned_changes:
- Keep the `retry` testcase branch on the managed child-process path in `InteropHarnessRunner`.
- Sequence the server to emit exactly one valid Retry and the client to replay the Initial exactly once on the real harness path.
- Advance the managed handshake only after the post-Retry path completes, then serve the runner-generated single HTTP/0.9 request through the existing mounted `/www` to `/downloads` contract.
- Keep the harness exit code truthful by returning `0` only after byte delivery plus EOF complete for the retry request.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for one-valid-Retry issuance, one-replay client behavior, successful post-Retry handshake completion, post-Retry byte delivery, and missing-file failure without widening broader transfer or public API scope.
completion_notes: The managed child-process path now dispatches `retry` through the managed one-Retry replay path, the server emits exactly one Retry, the client observes and replays it once, the post-Retry handshake completes, and the harness returns `0` only after the runner-generated single HTTP/0.9 request reaches byte delivery plus EOF.
out_of_scope: transfer generalization outside the retry testcase
verification_plan: Run the retry requirement-home tests, the local preflight retry smoke test, the helper-script regression tests for runner output classification, the library-owned retry bootstrap tests, and the current interop guards; then rerun the local quic-go retry helper and render/validate SpecTrace artifacts after canonical JSON changes.
